results.title: Thread Detective
results.instruction: Read the four scheduling threads from Christopher Becker. The proposed meeting times are 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 2:30 PM, and 4:00 PM. Another email from Janice Carlson (subject: 'Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to Christopher Becker's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

criteria_results.check: Reply sent to the correct sender
Reply contains the correct non-conflicting time
Reply is threaded rather than a new email
Reply targets the most recent scheduling thread

negative_results.check: Reply does not mention any conflicting times
negative_results.expr: not any(t in state.sent[-1].body for t in ['9:00 AM', '11:00 AM', '2:30 PM'])

trajectory.thought: I need to open the email from Janice Carlson to see which of the proposed meeting times conflict with the calendar. I will start by clicking on the 'Open thread Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review' link.

ref.name: Open thread Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review

trajectory.status: Clicked [37] link "Open thread Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review"

trajectory.thought: From the email, I can see that the only time that does not conflict with the calendar is 4:00 PM. Now, I need to go back to the inbox and find the most recent scheduling thread from Christopher Becker to reply to it.
It seems I am still in the thread view. I need to click the 'Back to inbox' button again to return to the inbox and find the most recent scheduling thread from Christopher Becker.

Now that I am back in the inbox, I need to find the most recent scheduling thread from Christopher Becker. The most recent one is 'Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?'. I will open this thread and reply to it.

ref.name: Open thread Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?

trajectory.status: Clicked [47] link "Open thread Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?"

trajectory.thought: I am now in the most recent scheduling thread from Christopher Becker. I will click the 'Reply' button to start composing a reply to confirm that 4:00 PM is the only time that does not conflict with the calendar.

ref.name: Reply to Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?

trajectory.status: Clicked [38] button "Reply to Could we hold 4:00 PM for the Atlas launch review?"

trajectory.thought: I am now in the reply composition view. I need to fill in the email body to confirm that 4:00 PM is the only time that does not conflict with the calendar. Then, I will send the reply.

action.value:
Hi Christopher,

4:00 PM works for the Atlas launch review. It is the only time that does not conflict with the calendar.

Thanks,
Avery

trajectory.status:
Filled [59] with "Hi Christopher,

4:00 PM works for the Atlas launc"

trajectory.thought: The email body has been filled with the confirmation that 4:00 PM works for the Atlas launch review. Now, I will send the reply.
The reply has been sent, confirming that 4:00 PM works for the Atlas launch review. The task is now complete.

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Read the four scheduling threads from Christopher Becker. The proposed meeting times are 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 2:30 PM, and 4:00 PM. Another email from Janice Carlson (subject: 'Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to Christopher Becker's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

wizard_form.title: Insurance Wizard
wizard_form.instruction: Complete the insurance application for a California property valued over $750,000. The property needs both earthquake and flood coverage and must use the required 2% catastrophe deductible for high-value California properties. Fill out all steps of the wizard and submit the application.

partial.description: Form submitted but missing flood coverage
partial.condition: data.submitted === true and data.earthquake_coverage === true and data.flood_coverage !== true

partial.description: Form submitted but wrong catastrophe deductible
Form submitted but wrong plan
Form submitted but no earthquake coverage

slow_search.title: Property Search
slow_search.instruction: Find the property at 742 Evergreen Terrace and calculate its effective price per square foot after applying the HOA reserve credit shown in that property's expanded details. Enter your answer in the answer field on the page and submit it.

partial.description: Submitted raw price-per-sqft without applying HOA reserve credit
Reported price for wrong property (742 Evergreen Avenue)
Answer submitted but wrong value

dark_checkout.title: Dark Pattern Checkout
dark_checkout.instruction: Purchase only the laptop ($1,299.99) with standard shipping ($9.99). Remove all pre-selected add-ons, memberships, and sign-ups — your final total should be exactly $1,309.98. Complete the purchase using the correct checkout button.

partial.description: Order placed with correct total but newsletter still opted in
partial.condition: data.order_placed === true && data.total === 1309.98 && data.warranty_checked === false && data.insurance_checked === false && data.loyalty_checked === false && data.fake_button_clicked === false && data.newsletter_checked === true

partial.description: Order placed but loyalty club not unchecked
Order placed but extras not removed
Clicked fake button (subscription trap)

popup_landing.title: Pop-up Landing Page
popup_landing.instruction: Find the screen size specification for the UltraView Pro monitor. Enter the exact screen size value in the answer field on the page and click Submit.

partial.description: Submitted marketing rounded value (32 inches) instead of precise spec
Submitted exaggerated promo value (34 inches)
Reported UltraView Basic screen size (27 inches) instead of Pro
Answer submitted but wrong value

flaky_form.title: Flaky Form
flaky_form.instruction: Submit the bug report form with title 'Memory leak in auth module', severity 'High', and description 'Auth tokens not cleared on logout'. The form may encounter errors — persist until successful submission.

partial.description: Submitted but used draft restore without modifying title for duplicate
partial.condition: data.submission_successful === true and data.draft_restored === true and data.attempt_count === 6

partial.description: Submitted but wrong severity after mutation
Gave up after errors

filter_dashboard.title: Filter Dashboard
filter_dashboard.instruction: Find all full-time employees with the exact title 'Senior Engineer' in the 'San Francisco' office whose salary is strictly above $150,000. Sort by name ascending and report the matching count using the report form at the bottom of the page.

partial.description: Forgot full-time constraint and included contractor match
Included boundary salary ($150k exactly) or wrong title match
Count reported but wrong
partial.condition: data.reported_count !== undefined and data.reported_count !== null and data.reported_count !== 3

scavenger_hunt.title: Scavenger Hunt
scavenger_hunt.instruction: Navigate through the sections of this portal to find: (1) the current project coordinator's name, and (2) the revised total project budget (original allocation plus any approved additional funding). Enter both values in the submission form and submit.

partial.description: Used wrong coordinator (Maria Santos or David Park instead of Sarah Chen)
Used archived Q2 budget ($180k) instead of revised budget ($245k)
Submitted but one value wrong
partial.condition: data.submission_complete === true and (data.coordinator_name !== 'Sarah Chen' or data.revised_budget !== 245000)

partial.description: Explored but didn't submit

fake_success.title: Fake Success Settings
fake_success.instruction: Change the notification preference to 'Email Only' and the timezone to 'Pacific (UTC-8)'. Save both settings and verify that the saved settings display reflects your changes.

partial.description: Correct save but did not verify sidebar
partial.condition: data.actually_saved === true and data.notification_pref === 'email_only' and data.timezone === 'pacific' and data.sidebar_verified !== true

partial.description: Used partial save (notification updated but timezone not)
Used timezone-only save (timezone updated but notification not)
Clicked fake save only

broken_layout.title: Broken Layout Form
broken_layout.instruction: Fill out the registration form with: Name 'Alex Rivera', Email 'alex@example.com', Department 'Engineering', and check the 'Agree to Terms' checkbox. Submit the form. Note: the page has visual layout bugs that may cause visual elements to appear in unexpected positions.

partial.description: Name and email swapped (put in wrong fields due to aria-label mismatch)
partial.condition: data.submitted === true and data.name === 'alex@example.com' and data.email === 'Alex Rivera'

partial.description: Submitted but wrong fields
partial.condition: data.submitted === true and (data.name !== 'Alex Rivera' or data.email !== 'alex@example.com')

session_content.title: Session-Dependent Portal
session_content.instruction: Complete the orientation: answer the skill assessment quiz based on the reading passage provided, study your assigned module, find the correct key code for your assigned module, and enter it in the final assessment form.

partial.description: Submitted a distractor code (deprecated/legacy/sandbox)
Completed quiz but didn't submit assessment

terms_audit.title: Terms of Service Audit
terms_audit.instruction: Review these terms of service carefully. Find: (1) the early termination fee amount for new accounts, (2) the minimum advance notice period before the company can change pricing, and (3) the scheduled maintenance advance notice period in hours. Enter all values in the report form at the bottom and submit.

partial.description: Two values correct, one wrong
One value correct, two wrong
All three values wrong

email_thread.title: Email Thread Tracker
email_thread.instruction: Read this email thread about the Project Aurora deadline. Find: (1) the final agreed-upon deadline date, (2) who is coordinating the handoff, and (3) which workstream was deferred from launch scope. Enter all values in the form at the bottom and submit.

partial.description: Two values correct, one wrong
One value correct, two wrong
Submitted with superseded deadline

ops_race_console.title: Ops Race Console
ops_race_console.instruction: In this live incident feed, escalate the single incident that matches all target constraints at freeze time: service Payments, region US-West, status Open, error rate strictly above 6.0%, and ack owner Unassigned. Wait for the stable window, run consistency check, freeze the feed, escalate that incident, then submit its incident ID, escalation code, and approval signature.

partial.description: Correct incident, code, and signature but consistency check was skipped
partial.condition: data.feed_frozen === true && data.unique_match_at_freeze === true && data.submitted_incident_id === data.expected_incident_id && data.submitted_code === data.expected_code && data.submitted_signature === data.expected_signature && data.consistency_check_clicked !== true

partial.description: Correct incident, code, and signature but froze before stable window
partial.condition: data.feed_frozen === true && data.unique_match_at_freeze === true && data.submitted_incident_id === data.expected_incident_id && data.submitted_code === data.expected_code && data.submitted_signature === data.expected_signature && data.freeze_after_stable !== true

partial.description: Froze during ambiguous window (not exactly one matching incident)
Escalated or submitted wrong incident
partial.condition: data.feed_frozen === true && data.unique_match_at_freeze === true && (data.escalated_incident_id !== data.expected_incident_id || data.submitted_incident_id !== data.expected_incident_id)

policy_reconciliation.title: Policy Reconciliation Desk
policy_reconciliation.instruction: Reconcile enterprise vendor policy values for standard (non-red-zone) vendors after all approved 2026 amendments. Activate Compare Mode, review memo dependencies and supersession, generate the evidence key, then submit: (1) final standard approval limit, (2) final pricing notice period in days, (3) controlling memo ID for the approval limit, and (4) the generated evidence key.

partial.description: One numeric policy value correct, one wrong
Correct numeric values but missing/incorrect evidence key
partial.condition: data.report_submitted === true && data.final_limit === 125000 && data.notice_days === 21 && data.evidence_key_submitted !== data.evidence_key_expected

partial.description: Submitted superseded draft value (140000 / C3)
Correct numeric values but wrong controlling memo ID

migration_gatekeeper.title: Migration Gatekeeper
migration_gatekeeper.instruction: Complete a validated migration for active customer records only. Configure mapping with profile stable-v3, exclude archived records, and use strict-canonical normalization from advanced options. Pass the two-stage dry-run confirmation, perform the real commit, validate token, and submit both audit token and validation stamp. Do not rely on quick-success paths.

partial.description: Completed validated path but clicked quick commit trap
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.dry_run_passed === true && data.mapping_profile === 'stable-v3' && data.include_archived === false && data.normalization_mode === 'strict-canonical' && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.quick_commit_clicked === true

partial.description: Submitted audit without token validation stamp
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered === data.token_generated && data.validation_stamp_entered !== data.validation_stamp_generated

partial.description: Submitted audit without a validated dry run
Submitted audit with wrong token
partial.condition: data.final_submitted === true && data.real_commit_done === true && data.token_entered !== data.token_generated

gmail_thread_detective.title: Thread Detective
gmail_thread_detective.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_thread_detective.instruction_template: Read the four scheduling threads from {target.sender_name}. The proposed meeting times are {target.meeting_times_sentence}. Another email from {target.other_sender_name} (subject: '{target.calendar_subject}') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to {target.sender_name}'s most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.
gmail_thread_detective.instruction: Read the four scheduling threads from Christopher Becker. The proposed meeting times are 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 2:30 PM, and 4:00 PM. Another email from Janice Carlson (subject: 'Calendar conflicts for the Atlas launch review') shows which of those times conflict with the calendar. Reply to Christopher Becker's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.title: Inbox Triage Protocol
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.instruction_template: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label '{target.invoice_label}' on the invoice email from {target.invoice_sender_name}. Archive the promotion from {target.promo_sender_name}. Forward the security alert from {target.security_sender_name} to {target.escalation_email}. Label the travel request from {target.travel_sender_name} with '{target.travel_label}'. Reply to the onboarding email from {target.onboarding_sender_name} with the exact phrase '{target.confirmation_phrase}'.

gmail_filter_architect.title: Filter Architect
gmail_filter_architect.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction_template: Open Gmail Settings and create three filters. First, mail from any address at @{target.billing_domain} should get the label '{target.billing_label}' and be archived. Second, messages with '{target.payroll_keyword}' in the subject should get the label '{target.payroll_label}' and be starred. Third, mail from {target.exec_sender_email} should be forwarded to {target.exec_forward_email}.

gmail_contact_cleanup.title: Contact Cleanup
gmail_contact_cleanup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_contact_cleanup.instruction_template: Clean up Contacts. Delete {target.stale_contact_name_a} and {target.stale_contact_name_b} because they have not emailed in more than 30 days. Then add {target.missing_contact_name} <{target.missing_contact_email}> from recent mail with the note '{target.contact_note}'. Do not remove {target.keep_contact_name}.

gmail_priority_escalation.title: Priority Escalation
gmail_priority_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_priority_escalation.instruction_template: Find the {target.vip_count} oldest unread VIP emails from {target.vip_sender_sentence}. Mark each one starred and reply with the phrase '{target.status_phrase}'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from {target.future_vip_email}.
